GETTY MARROW UNDERGRADUATE INTERNS AT THE GETTY

RlnOfActyToAccomOfExmptPrpsGrp.RelationshipStatementTxt: EACH FALL, VILLA OUTDOOR THEATER SERIES FEATURES A CLASSICAL TRAGEDY OR COMEDY AT THE GETTY VILLA THEATER, AN OUTDOOR THEATER BASED ON ANCIENT PROTOTYPES. IN ADDITION, THE GETTY PUTS ON THE VILLA THEATER LAB SERIES, WHICH FEATURES REINTERPRETATIONS OF CLASSICAL THEATER.
THE J. PAUL GETTY MUSEUM PROVIDES A VARIETY OF PROFESSIONAL DEVELOPMENT WORKSHOPS AND TEACHER COLLABORATION WORKSHOPS TO HELP EDUCATORS AND LEADERS INTEGRATE ART EDUCATION INTO THEIR ACTIVITIES.

ROYALTIES ARE RECEIVED FROM THREE SOURCES: (1) ROYALTIES ARE RECEIVED FROM PROQUEST, THE INFORMATION COMPANY THAT HAS CONTRACTED WITH THE GRI TO PROVIDE ACCESS TO THE LEGACY DATA FROM THE FORMER GRI PROJECT, THE INTERNATIONAL BIBLIOGRAPHY OF ART, WHICH PROVIDED COMPREHENSIVE ENGLISH-LANGUAGE INDEXING AND ABSTRACTING FOR ARTICLES, BOOKS AND CATALOGS ON WESTERN ART. (2) WORKING WITH THE COPYRIGHT CLEARANCE CENTER (CCC), GRI PUBLICATIONS LICENSES ITS CONTENT TO UNIVERSITIES FOR USE IN COURSE PACKETS AND TO PUBLISHERS IN THE UNITED STATES AND ABROAD WHO WANT TO REPRINT GRI SCHOLARSHIP. (3) THE GETTY RECEIVED ROYALTIES FROM ADA LOUISE HUXTABLE'S PUBLICATIONS.

THE GETTY HOSTS EDUCATIONAL EVENTS FOR CERTAIN LOCAL, NATIONAL AND INTERNATIONAL ORGANIZATIONS OPERATING IN THE FIELDS RELATING TO THE GETTY'S MISSION IN ORDER TO FURTHER THE GETTY'S OWN PRIORITIES AND AS A SERVICE TO ORGANIZATIONS WITH COMMON VALUES AND INTERESTS OR WITH OBJECTIVES THAT MEET THE PUBLIC NEEDS. UPON REQUEST, THE MUSEUM AND THE RESEARCH INSTITUTE MAKE AVAILABLE PHOTOGRAPHS AND SLIDES OF WORKS OF ART IN ITS COLLECTIONS. THESE PHOTOGRAPHS ARE PROVIDED FREE OF CHARGE TO SCHOLARS AND ARTS INSTITUTIONS. A NOMINAL CHARGE IS MADE FOR OTHER REQUESTS TO OFFSET PRODUCTION COSTS.

THE MUSEUM BOOKSTORE MAKES AVAILABLE TO THE PUBLIC A WIDE VARIETY OF GENERAL ART BOOKS AS WELL AS PUBLICATIONS, PRINTS AND POSTCARDS SPECIFIC TO THE MUSEUM'S COLLECTIONS. ADDITIONALLY, SCHOLARLY PUBLICATIONS PRODUCED BY J. PAUL GETTY TRUST PUBLICATIONS, THE RESEARCH INSTITUTE AND THE CONSERVATION INSTITUTE ARE AVAILABLE FROM THE BOOKSTORE.

GeneralExplanationGrp.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: PART VI-B, LINE 5A (D) - EXPENDITURE RESPONSIBILTY GRANTS
GeneralExplanationGrp.MediumExplanationTxt: IN ACCORDANCE WITH IRS REGULATION 53.4945-5(D)(2), THE FOLLOWING INFORMATION IS PROVIDED REGARDING GRANTS MADE OR OUTSTANDING DURING THE YEAR TO ORGANIZATIONS NOT QUALIFYING UNDER IRC 509(A)(1), 509(A)(2), 509(A)(3) OR 4940(D)(2). FOR EACH EXPENDITURE RESPONSIBILITY GRANT, THE (1) NAME AND ADDRESS OF THE GRANTEE, (2) DATE AND AMOUNT OF THE GRANT, (3) PURPOSE OF THE GRANT, (4) AMOUNTS EXPENDED BY THE GRANTEE (BASED ON THE MOST RECENT REPORT RECEIVED FROM THE GRANTEE), (5) DATES OF ANY REPORTS RECEIVED FROM THE GRANTEE, (6) DATE AND RESULTS OF ANY VERIFICATION OF THE GRANTEE'S REPORTS UNDERTAKEN BY OR AT THE DIRECTION OF THE J. PAUL GETTY TRUST AND (7) WHETHER, TO THE KNOWLEDGE OF THE J. PAUL GETTY TRUST, IF THE GRANTEE HAS DIVERTED ANY FUNDS FROM THE PURPOSE OF THE GRANT. NOTE, TO THE BEST OF OUR KNOWLEDGE AND BELIEF, THERE WERE NO DIVERSIONS OF GRANT FUNDS BY ANY GRANTEE FOR ANY USE NOT IN FURTHERANCE OF A PURPOSE SPECIFIED IN THE GRANT. INFORMATION REGARDING AMOUNTS EXPENDED BY THE GRANTEE AND RELATED REPORTS MAY NOT BE AVAILABLE AT THE TIME OF FILING THIS RETURN IF A GRANT HAS NOT YET BEEN PAID OR IF SCHEDULED REPORTS ARE NOT YET DUE.

GeneralExplanationGrp.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: PART VII - INFORMATION REGARDING DEFERRED COMPENSATION
GeneralExplanationGrp.MediumExplanationTxt: THE J. PAUL GETTY TRUST PROVIDES AN INVESTMENT INCENTIVE AND BONUS PLAN TO CERTAIN EMPLOYEES. ACCRUALS UNDER THIS PLAN ARE REPORTED AS DEFERRED COMPENSATION ON FORM 990-PF, PART VII, COLUMN (D). WHEN AMOUNTS UNDER THIS PLAN ARE NO LONGER SUBJECT TO A SUBSTANTIAL RISK OF FORFEITURE, THESE AMOUNTS WILL BE REPORTED AGAIN AS COMPENSATION OF PART VII, COLUMN (C).

GeneralExplanationGrp.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: PART VIII-A SUMMARY OF DIRECT CHARITABLE ACTIVITIES LINE 1
GeneralExplanationGrp.MediumExplanationTxt: THE J. PAUL GETTY MUSEUMTHE J. PAUL GETTY MUSEUM COLLECTS GREEK AND ROMAN ANTIQUITIES, EUROPEAN PAINTINGS, DRAWINGS, MANUSCRIPTS, SCULPTURE AND DECORATIVE ARTS TO 1900, AS WELL AS PHOTOGRAPHS FROM AROUND THE WORLD TO THE PRESENT DAY. THE MUSEUM'S MISSION IS TO DISPLAY AND INTERPRET ITS COLLECTIONS AND PRESENT IMPORTANT LOAN EXHIBITIONS AND PUBLICATIONS FOR THE ENJOYMENT AND EDUCATION OF VISITORS LOCALLY AND INTERNATIONALLY. THIS IS SUPPORTED BY AN ACTIVE PROGRAM OF RESEARCH, CONSERVATION, AND PUBLIC PROGRAMS THAT SEEK TO DEEPEN OUR KNOWLEDGE OF AND CONNECTION TO WORKS OF ART. THE MUSEUM OPERATES TWO SITES IN LOS ANGELES - ONE AT THE GETTY CENTER; THE SECOND AT THE GETTY VILLA. FOR MORE INFORMATION AND TO SEE RECENT HIGHLIGHTS AND PAST EXHIBITIONS, VISIT GETTY.EDU.EXHIBITIONSWILLIAM BLAKE: VISIONARYA REMARKABLE PRINTMAKER, PAINTER, AND POET, WILLIAM BLAKE (1757-1827) DEVELOPED A WILDLY UNCONVENTIONAL WORLD VIEW, REPRESENTING UNIVERSAL FORCES OF CREATION AND DESTRUCTION - PHYSICAL, PSYCHOLOGICAL, HISTORICAL - THROUGH HIS OWN CAST OF CHARACTERS. BY COMBINING HIS POETRY AND IMAGES ON THE PAGE THROUGH RADICAL GRAPHIC TECHNIQUES, BLAKE CREATED SOME OF THE MOST STRIKING AND ENDURING IMAGERY IN BRITISH ART. THIS MAJOR INTERNATIONAL LOAN EXHIBITION EXPLORED THE ARTIST-POET'S IMAGINATIVE WORLD THROUGH HIS MOST CELEBRATED WORKS.HTTPS://WWW.GETTY.EDU/ART/EXHIBITIONS/BLAKE/INDEX.HTMLCAMILLE CLAUDELCELEBRATED FOR HER BRILLIANCE DURING A TIME WHEN WOMEN SCULPTORS WERE RARE, CAMILLE CLAUDEL (1864-1943) WAS AMONG THE MOST DARING AND VISIONARY ARTISTS OF THE LATE 19TH CENTURY. ALTHOUGH SHE IS REMEMBERED TODAY FOR HER DRAMATIC LIFE STORY HER PASSIONATE RELATIONSHIP WITH ARTIST AUGUSTE RODIN AND 30-YEAR INTERNMENT IN A PSYCHIATRIC INSTITUTION HER ART REMAINS LITTLE KNOWN OUTSIDE OF FRANCE. INCLUDING ABOUT 60 SCULPTURES, THIS MAJOR EXHIBITION SOUGHT TO REEVALUATE CLAUDEL'S WORK AND AFFIRM HER LEGACY WITHIN A MORE COMPLEX GENEALOGY OF MODERNISM.HTTPS://WWW.GETTY.EDU/ART/EXHIBITIONS/CLAUDEL/INDEX.HTMLPICTURE WORLDS: GREEK, MAYA, AND MOCHE POTTERYMIGHTY DEITIES, BRAVE HEROES, AND FANTASTIC BEINGS ADORN THE TERRACOTTA VESSELS OF THE ANCIENT GREEKS IN THE MEDITERRANEAN, THE MAYA IN CENTRAL AMERICA, AND THE MOCHE OF NORTHERN PERU. THIS EXHIBITION JUXTAPOSED THESE THREE DISTINCT CERAMIC TRADITIONS AND EXPLORED THE WAYS IN WHICH PAINTED POTTERY SERVED AS A DYNAMIC MEANS OF STORYTELLING AND SOCIAL ENGAGEMENT.HTTPS://WWW.GETTY.EDU/ART/EXHIBITIONS/PICTURE_WORLDS/INDEX.HTMLEDUCATION AND PUBLIC PROGRAMSTHE GOSPEL AT COLONUSTHE 17TH ANNUAL OUTDOOR CLASSICAL THEATER PRODUCTION AT THE GETTY VILLA. BASED ON SOPHOCLES'S OEDIPUS AT COLONUS FROM THE 5TH CENTURY BCE AND CO-PRODUCED BY COURT THEATRE, THIS ADAPTATION BLENDED GREEK MYTH WITH BLACK SPIRITUAL PRACTICE FOR A JUBILANT, LIFE-AFFIRMING JOURNEY.HTTPS://WWW.GETTY.EDU/VISIT/CAL/EVENTS/EV_3880.HTMLEVER PRESENTTHREE ARTISTS NAVIGATED THEIR RELATIONSHIPS TO THE NATURAL WORLD, TO SHIFTING INTENSITIES ACROSS THE PHASES OF LIFE, AND TO INHABITING A COLLISION OF CULTURES IN THIS AFTERNOON OF PERFORMANCES THAT ENGAGE WITH LANGUAGE, PSYCHOLOGY, AND SOUND. JJJJJEROME ELLIS PRESENTED A SITE-SPECIFIC PERFORMANCE OF POETRY AND MUSIC IN THE CENTRAL GARDEN. KERRY TRIBE STAGED A NEW WORK TITLED "THE COMPLAINT." MARAL PERFORMED A UNIQUE MUSICAL SET FEATURING SPECIAL GUESTS ON THE GARDEN TERRACE STAGE.HTTPS://WWW.GETTY.EDU/VISIT/CAL/EVENTS/EV_3948.HTMLWELLNESS DAY FOR EDUCATORSAN INCREDIBLE DAY OF WELLNESS ACTIVITIES GEARED SPECIALLY FOR EDUCATORS AT THE SCENIC GETTY CENTER. THE DAY INCLUDED ACTIVITIES SUCH AS A HEALING SOUND BATH, ARTMAKING, GUIDED MINDFULNESS, YOGA, AND TIME TO RECHARGE AND CONNECT WITH FELLOW MEMBERS OF THE EDUCATION COMMUNITY.HTTPS://WWW.GETTY.EDU/VISIT/CAL/EVENTS/EV_3824.HTMLCONSERVATIONHERCULES AND OMPHALETHE LARGE-SCALE OIL PAINTING HERCULES AND OMPHALE BY ITALIAN PAINTER ARTEMISIA GENTILESCHI CAME TO GETTY FOR RESTORATION AFTER IT WAS EXTENSIVELY DAMAGED BY SHRAPNEL IN THE DEADLY PORT EXPLOSION IN BEIRUT IN 2020. GENTILESCHI MADE THE PAINTING AROUND 1630. THE PAINTING HAD BEEN PREVIOUSLY UNKNOWN, AS IT WAS IN A PRIVATE COLLECTION HELD IN SURSOCK PALACE. AFTER ITS RESTORATION BY MUSEUM CONSERVATORS, THE PAINTING WILL GO ON VIEW AT GETTY IN SUMMER 2025 BEFORE RETURNING TO BEIRUT.HTTPS://WWW.GETTY.EDU/NEWS/GETTY-MUSEUM-CONSERVES-RECENTLY-REDISCOVERED-PAINTING-BY-ARTEMISIA-GENTILESCHI/ACQUISITIONSGERARD DAVID(NETHERLANDS, OUDEWATER, CA. 1455 - BRUGES 1523)THE HOLY FAMILY, ABOUT 1520OIL ON PANEL, 40.9 X 33 CM (16 1/8 X 13 IN.)HTTPS://WWW.GETTY.EDU/ART/COLLECTION/OBJECT/1149BM ANTON RAPHAEL MENGS(GERMAN, AUSSIG 1728 - 1779 ROME)PORTRAIT OF FREDRICH CHRISTIAN, PRINCE OF SAXONY, 1751OIL ON CANVAS, 61 1/4 X 43 1/4 IN. (155.7 X 110.8 CM)HTTPS://WWW.GETTY.EDU/ART/COLLECTION/OBJECT/1149AS LUDGER TOM RING THE YOUNGER(MUNSTER 1522 - BRAUNSCHWEIG 1584)BOUQUET OF FLOWERS IN A TWO-HANDLED VASE, EARLY 1560SOIL ON OAK PANEL, 14 15/16 X 11 7/16 IN. (38 X 29 CM)HTTPS://WWW.GETTY.EDU/ART/COLLECTION/OBJECT/1149BK A COLLECTION OF 17 DRAWINGSOFFERED PRIVATELY BY A COLLECTOR IN LONDON, ENGLANDINCLUDING WORKS BY: PAUL-ALBERT BESNARD, GIOVANNI BOLDINI, LUCA CAMBIASO, THOMAS COUTURE, EDGAR DEGAS, GAETANO GANDOLFI, EVA GONZALES, GIOVANNI FRANCESCO BARBIERI, HUGH DOUGLAS HAMILTON, PIETER HOLSTEYN THE YOUNGER, FRANCESCO PAOLO MICHETTI, ODILON REDON, LUIGI SABATELLI, JOAQUIN SOROLLA Y BASTIDA, JAN VAN DER STRAET, CALLED STRADANUS, AND JOSEPH WRIGHT OF DERBY.HTTPS://WWW.GETTY.EDU/NEWS/GETTY-ACQUIRES-17-REMARKABLE-DRAWINGS/ COMMUNITY ENGAGEMENT PROJECTSCAMILLE CLAUDELTHIS PAST SPRING, GETTY PRODUCED A COMMUNITY ENGAGEMENT WORKSHOP BASED ON THE SCULPTURE EXHIBITION CAMILLE CLAUDEL. THIS DEBUT PARTNERSHIP BETWEEN GETTY, WALLIS ANNENBERG GENSPACE - A SENIOR COMMUNITY CENTER, AND THE FEMINIST CENTER FOR CREATIVE WORK (FCCW), INTRODUCED OLDER ANGELENOS TO CLAUDEL'S WORK AND TAUGHT THEM SCULPTURE-MAKING. PARTICIPANTS ENGAGED IN A SIX-WEEK COURSE, INCLUDING A FIELD TRIP TO THE GETTY CENTER AND CLAY SCULPTING SESSIONS. LED BY FCCW'S TEACHING ARTIST SILVI NACI, THE COURSE ENCOURAGED PARTICIPANTS TO EXPRESS PERSONAL AND HISTORICAL STRUGGLES THROUGH THEIR SCULPTURES, FOSTERING CONNECTION, SELF-EXPRESSION, AND REFLECTION ON THE CHALLENGES FACED BY WOMEN THROUGHOUT HISTORY.HTTPS://WWW.GETTY.EDU/NEWS/A-THOUSAND-CUTS/ PICTURE WORLDS: GREEK, MAYA, AND MOCHE POTTERYIN SPRING 2024, GETTY COLLABORATED WITH TWO PROMINENT NONPROFIT ORGANIZATIONS TO ENGAGE LOCAL COMMUNITIES AND PROMOTE THE EXHIBITION PICTURE WORLDS: GREEK, MAYA, AND MOCHE POTTERY. THE EVENTS TOOK PLACE AT BOB BAKER MARIONETTE THEATER'S "BOB BAKER DAY AND TIA CHUCHA'S "CELEBRATING WORDS FESTIVAL," WHICH EACH ATTRACTED THOUSANDS OF ATTENDEES, MANY OF WHOM PARTICIPATED IN GETTY'S ACTIVITIES AND RECEIVED FREE PARKING PASSES TO THE GETTY VILLA AND GETTY CENTER. THE EVENTS INCLUDED ART WORKSHOPS LED BY ARTIST MARCUS KUILAND-NAZARIO, WHERE PARTICIPANTS CREATED UNIQUE ARTWORKS INSPIRED BY ANCIENT POTTERY TRADITIONS, FOSTERING CROSS-CULTURAL UNDERSTANDING AND CREATIVITY. BY PARTICIPATING IN THESE FESTIVALS, GETTY CONNECTED WITH DIVERSE, MULTI-GENERATIONAL COMMUNITIES ACROSS LOS ANGELES COUNTY, PARTICULARLY IN NEIGHBORHOODS TRADITIONALLY UNDERSERVED BY THE INSTITUTION.

GeneralExplanationGrp.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: PART VIII-A SUMMARY OF DIRECT CHARITABLE ACTIVITIES LINE 2
GeneralExplanationGrp.MediumExplanationTxt: THE GETTY RESEARCH INSTITUTETHE GETTY RESEARCH INSTITUTE IS AN OPERATING PROGRAM OF THE J. PAUL GETTY TRUST. IT SERVES EDUCATION IN THE BROADEST SENSE BY INCREASING KNOWLEDGE AND UNDERSTANDING ABOUT ART AND ITS HISTORY THROUGH ADVANCED RESEARCH. THE RESEARCH INSTITUTE PROVIDES INTELLECTUAL LEADERSHIP THROUGH ITS RESEARCH, EXHIBITION, AND PUBLICATION PROGRAMS AND PROVIDES SERVICE TO A WIDE RANGE OF RESEARCHERS WORLDWIDE THROUGH RESIDENCIES, FELLOWSHIPS, ONLINE RESOURCES, AND THE GETTY LIBRARY. THE GETTY LIBRARY-HOUSED IN THE 201,000-SQUARE-FOOT RESEARCH INSTITUTE-IS ONE OF THE LARGEST ART AND ARCHITECTURE LIBRARIES IN THE WORLD. THE GENERAL LIBRARY COLLECTIONS (SECONDARY SOURCES) INCLUDE OVER 1.5 MILLION VOLUMES OF BOOKS, PERIODICALS, AND AUCTION CATALOGUES ENCOMPASSING THE HISTORY OF WESTERN ART AND RELATED FIELDS IN THE HUMANITIES. THE GETTY LIBRARY'S SPECIAL COLLECTIONS INCLUDE RARE BOOKS, ARTISTS' JOURNALS, SKETCHBOOKS, ARCHITECTURAL DRAWINGS AND MODELS, PHOTOGRAPHS, AND ARCHIVAL MATERIALS. GeneralExplanationGrp.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: PART VIII-A SUMMARY OF DIRECT CHARITABLE ACTIVITIES LINE 3
GeneralExplanationGrp.MediumExplanationTxt: THE GETTY CONSERVATION INSTITUTETHE GETTY CONSERVATION INSTITUTE (GCI) IS AN OPERATING PROGRAM OF THE J. PAUL GETTY TRUST. THE GCI WORKS INTERNATIONALLY TO ADVANCE CONSERVATION PRACTICE IN THE VISUAL ARTS-BROADLY INTERPRETED TO INCLUDE OBJECTS, COLLECTIONS, ARCHITECTURE, AND SITES. THE INSTITUTE SERVES THE CONSERVATION COMMUNITY THROUGH SCIENTIFIC RESEARCH, EDUCATION AND TRAINING, FIELD PROJECTS, AND THE DISSEMINATION OF INFORMATION. IN ALL ITS ENDEAVORS, THE GCI CREATES AND DELIVERS KNOWLEDGE THAT CONTRIBUTES TO THE CONSERVATION OF THE WORLD'S CULTURAL HERITAGE.FOR MORE INFORMATION AND TO READ MORE ABOUT SOME OF THE PAST FISCAL YEAR HIGHLIGHTS, VISIT GETTY.EDU:CONSERVING MODERN ARCHITECTURE INITIATIVE (CMAI)DURING THE SUMMER OF 2023, 26 MODERN HERITAGE PROFESSIONALS FROM 23 COUNTRIES VISITED GETTY FOR THE FINAL 2 WEEKS OF AN 11 WEEK HYBRID COURSE, AS PART OF CMAI'S INTERNATIONAL COURSE ON THE CONSERVATION OF MODERN HERITAGE. THE COURSE WAS DESIGNED TO ADDRESS THE ISSUE OF MODERN HERITAGE CONSERVATION, AND PROVIDED PARTICIPANTS WITH THE OPPORTUNITY TO CONNECT WITH EACH OTHER AND LEARN FROM LEADING EXPERTS IN THE FIELD.HTTPS://WWW.GETTY.EDU/NEWS/CONSERVING-MODERN-ARCHITECTURE-INITATIVE-GETTY-COURSE/RECENT ADVANCES IN CHARACTERIZING ASIAN LACQUER (RADICAL) WORKSHOPTHE RECENT ADVANCES IN CHARACTERIZING ASIAN LACQUER (RADICAL) WORKSHOP, HELD IN APRIL 2024, WAS CO-ORGANIZED WITH THE PALACE MUSEUM, BEIJING, AND WAS ADAPTED TO CHINA'S SPECIFIC WORK ENVIRONMENT. THE FIVE-DAY WORKSHOP EXPLORED NEWLY DEVELOPED ANALYTICAL PROCEDURES FOR ACQUIRING DETAILED COMPOSITIONAL INFORMATION ABOUT ASIAN LACQUERS, THEIR ADDITIVES, AND THEIR EUROPEAN SUBSTITUTES. HTTPS://WWW.GETTY.EDU/PROJECTS/RECENT-ADVANCES-CHARACTERIZING-ASIAN-LACQUER/RADICAL-WORKSHOP-2024/CHANGING CLIMATE MANAGEMENT STRATEGIES WORKSHOPGETTY CONSERVATION INSTITUTE'S MANAGING COLLECTION ENVIRONMENTS (MCE) INITIATIVE ADDRESSES THE SUSTAINABLE MANAGEMENT OF EXHIBITION, STORAGE, AND TRANSIT ENVIRONMENTS FOR MUSEUMS, GALLERIES, ARCHIVES, AND LIBRARIES. THE 5 DAY WORKSHOP WAS CO-ORGANIZED WITH THE VICTORIA AND ALBERT MUSEUM, AND HELD IN LONDON DURING FEBRUARY 2024. IT ADDRESSED OBSTACLES IN DEVELOPING AND IMPLEMENTING MORE ADAPTIVE ENVIRONMENTAL MANAGEMENT STRATEGIES IN MUSEUMS. HTTPS://WWW.GETTY.EDU/PROJECTS/TRAINING-FOR-SUSTAINABLE-COLLECTION-CARE/CHANGING-CLIMATE-MANAGEMENT-STRATEGIES-WORKSHOP/THE GETTY CONSERVATION INSTITUTE PRODUCES A VARIETY OF PUBLICATIONS ON THE TOPICS OF SCIENTIFIC RESEARCH, CONFERENCE PROCEEDINGS, CASE STUDIES, PROJECT REPORTS, BIBLIOGRAPHIES AND WORKS ON ASPECTS OF CONSERVATION PRACTICES. SOME PUBLICATIONS ARE AVAILABLE FOR PURCHASE AND OTHERS ARE AVAILABLE AT GETTY.EDU/CONSERVATION. IN FY24, THESE PUBLICATIONS INCLUDED:HTTPS://WWW.GETTY.EDU/CONSERVATION/PUBLICATIONS_RESOURCES/PDF_PUBLICATIONS/MCE-TECNICAL-NOTES-AND-GUIDANCE.HTMLHTTPS://WWW.GETTY.EDU/CONSERVATION/PUBLICATIONS_RESOURCES/PDF_PUBLICATIONS/GREENER-SOLVENTS-IN-ART-CONSERVATION.HTMLHTTPS://WWW.GETTY.EDU/CONSERVATION/PUBLICATIONS_RESOURCES/PDF_PUBLICATIONS/BULLA-REGIA-MOSAIC-CONSERVATION-PROJECT.HTMLHTTPS://WWW.GETTY.EDU/CONSERVATION/PUBLICATIONS_RESOURCES/PDF_PUBLICATIONS/EAMES-HOUSE-CONSERVATION-PROJECT-CEMESTO-PANEL.HTMLHTTPS://SHOP.GETTY.EDU/COLLECTIONS/GETTY-PUBLICATIONS/PRODUCTS/INVENTORIES-AND-SURVEYS-FOR-HERITAGE-MANAGEMENT-LESSONS-FOR-THE-DIGITAL-AGE-9781606068816

GeneralExplanationGrp.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: PART VIII-A SUMMARY OF DIRECT CHARITABLE ACTIVITIES LINE 4
GeneralExplanationGrp.MediumExplanationTxt: THE GETTY FOUNDATIONTHE GETTY FOUNDATION FULFILLS THE PHILANTHROPIC MISSION OF THE GETTY TRUST BY SUPPORTING INDIVIDUALS AND INSTITUTIONS COMMITTED TO ADVANCING THE GREATER UNDERSTANDING AND PRESERVATION OF THE VISUAL ARTS IN LOS ANGELES AND THROUGHOUT THE WORLD. THROUGH STRATEGIC GRANT INITIATIVES, IT STRENGTHENS ART HISTORY AS A GLOBAL DISCIPLINE, PROMOTES THE INTERDISCIPLINARY PRACTICE OF CONSERVATION, INCREASES ACCESS TO MUSEUM AND ARCHIVAL COLLECTIONS, AND DEVELOPS CURRENT AND FUTURE LEADERS IN THE VISUAL ARTS. IT CARRIES OUT ITS WORK IN COLLABORATION WITH THE OTHER GETTY PROGRAMS TO ENSURE THAT THEY INDIVIDUALLY AND COLLECTIVELY ACHIEVE MAXIMUM EFFECT. TO DATE, THE FOUNDATION HAS DEVELOPED, ASSESSED, AWARDED, AND MONITORED OVER 9,000 GRANTS IN MORE THAN 180 COUNTRIES. IN FY24 THE FOUNDATION AWARDED 200 "CORE" GRANTS, WHICH INCLUDES 50 GRANTS FOR ONSITE GRADUATE AND UNDERGRADUATE INTERNSHIPS. IN ADDITION, THE FOUNDATION PROCESSED AND AWARDED 204 MATCHING GIFT GRANTS AND 106 GRANTS ON BEHALF OF THE J. PAUL GETTY TRUST AND GETTY'S OPERATING PROGRAMS (THE GETTY RESEARCH INSTITUTE, THE J. PAUL GETTY MUSEUM, AND THE GETTY CONSERVATION INSTITUTE) FOR SCHOLARS TO COMPLETE RESEARCH AT GETTY AND SPECIAL PROJECTS. NEW PST ART GRANTS FOR EXPANDED PUBLIC PROGRAMMING AND K-12 EDUCATIONGETTY SUPPORT FOR PST ART, A REGIONWIDE COLLABORATION OF THEMATICALLY-LINKED EXHIBITIONS AND PROGRAMS, ROSE TO OVER $19 MILLION WITH NEW GRANTS FOR DYNAMIC PERFORMING ARTS PROGRAMMING, COMMUNITY-BASED EVENTS, AND AN ART AND SCIENCE FAMILY FESTIVAL. WE ALSO AWARDED FUNDING TO THE LA PROMISE FUND AND GREATER LA EDUCATION FUND TO SUPPORT K-12 ARTS EDUCATION FOR STUDENTS AND FAMILIES THROUGHOUT LA COUNTY AND RELATED TEACHER PROFESSIONAL DEVELOPMENT AND CLASSROOM ACTIVITIES. HTTPS://WWW.GETTY.EDU/NEWS/GETTY-ANNOUNCES-EXPANSIVE-PUBLIC-PROGRAMMING-FOR-PST-ART-ART-SCIENCE-COLLIDE/ GETTY AWARDS OVER $1 MILLION FOR FINAL ROUND OF PAPER PROJECT GRANTS THIRTEEN NEW GRANTS FOR DIVERSE TRAINING OPPORTUNITIES SUPPORTING CURATORIAL INNOVATION IN THE GRAPHIC ARTS CLOSED OUT FUNDING FOR THE PAPER PROJECT. THIS INTERNATIONAL INITIATIVE HAS SUPPORTED AMBITIOUS CURATORS OF PRINTS AND DRAWINGS COLLECTIONS WHO WANT TO RAISE THE VISIBILITY OF WORKS ON PAPER. FUNDING HAS RANGED FROM PROJECT GRANTS AND FELLOWSHIPS TO PROFESSIONAL TRAINING PROGRAMS, ALL HOSTED BY 72 INSTITUTIONS WORLDWIDE. HTTPS://WWW.GETTY.EDU/NEWS/GETTY-AWARDS-OVER-1-MILLION-TO-FUND-CURATORIAL-INNOVATION/ CONSERVING CANVAS TRAINING PROGRAMS CONTINUEA SUITE OF RECENT CONSERVATION GRANTS ARE HELPING CONSERVATORS LEARN HOW TO CARE FOR PAINTINGS DAMAGED BY DISASTERS OR CONFLICTS. THE TRAINING PROGRAMS ARE PART OF CONSERVING CANVAS, AN INITIATIVE FOCUSED ON THE CONSERVATION OF PAINTINGS ON CANVAS THAT HAS NOW FUNDED OVER 35 GRANTS TO INSTITUTIONS IN EUROPE, THE UNITED STATES, AND LATIN AMERICA SINCE 2018.HTTPS://WWW.GETTY.EDU/NEWS/KEEP-CALM-AND-CARE-ON/ EIGHT ENDANGERED BUILDINGS DESIGNED BY BLACK ARCHITECTS RECEIVE GETTY SUPPORT2024 MARKED THE SECOND YEAR OF GRANTS FOR CONSERVING BLACK MODERNISM. ADMINISTERED BY THE NATIONAL TRUST FOR HISTORIC PRESERVATION AS PART OF ITS AFRICAN AMERICAN CULTURAL HERITAGE ACTION FUND, THE INITIATIVE ADDRESSES THE HISTORICAL OMISSIONS OF BUILDINGS DESIGNED BY BLACK ARCHITECTS FROM THE STORY OF MODERNISM IN THE UNITED STATES. THE FOUNDATION'S SUPPORT IS BEING USED FOR CONSERVATION PLANNING, PROFESSIONAL TRAINING, AND STORYTELLING TO CELEBRATE AND PRESERVE BUILDINGS BY BLACK ARCHITECTS FOR THE FUTURE.HTTPS://WWW.GETTY.EDU/NEWS/EIGHT-ENDANGERED-BUILDINGS-DESIGNED-BY-BLACK-ARCHITECTS-AWARDED-GETTY-FUNDING/ GETTY MARROW INTERNSHIPS AND EMERGING PROFESSIONALSFOR OVER THIRTY YEARS GETTY HAS INTRODUCED STUDENTS FROM UNDERREPRESENTED GROUPS TO CAREERS IN THE VISUAL ARTS THROUGH OVER 100 PAID SUMMER INTERNSHIPS AT GETTY AND CULTURAL ORGANIZATIONS ACROSS LOS ANGELES. WITH OVER 3,500 ALUMNI AND COUNTING, THE PROGRAM IS THE LARGEST OF ITS KIND NATIONALLY AND HAS BEEN SUCCESSFUL IN PROPELLING A THIRD OF ITS PARTICIPANTS INTO JOBS IN THE FIELD. GETTY MARROW EMERGING PROFESSIONALS IS A NEW PILOT PROGRAM THAT PROVIDES INTERNSHIP ALUMNI WITH MEANINGFUL ENTRY-LEVEL EMPLOYMENT AND MENTORING THAT ARE CRUCIAL TO BUILDING LASTING CAREERS. THE PROGRAM LAUNCHED IN 2022 AND RENEWAL GRANTS THIS FISCAL YEAR HAVE EXTENDED POSITIONS FOR A THIRD YEAR, AFTER WHICH THE FOUNDATION WILL ASSESS THE PROGRAM'S IMPACT.HTTPS://WWW.GETTY.EDU/NEWS/GETTY-MARROW-EMERGING-PROFESSIONALS-PILOT-PROGRAM-REFLECTIONS/

GeneralExplanationGrp.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: PART XIV, LINE 2 INFORMATION REGARDING GRANT PROGRAMS
GeneralExplanationGrp.MediumExplanationTxt: THE GETTY FOUNDATIONTHE GETTY FOUNDATION FULFILLS THE PHILANTHROPIC MISSION OF THE J. PAUL GETTY TRUST BY SUPPORTING INDIVIDUALS AND INSTITUTIONS COMMITTED TO ADVANCING THE UNDERSTANDING AND PRESERVATION OF THE VISUAL ARTS LOCALLY AND THROUGHOUT THE WORLD. THROUGH STRATEGIC GRANTS AND PROGRAMS, THE FOUNDATION STRENGTHENS ART HISTORY AS A GLOBAL DISCIPLINE, PROMOTES THE INTERDISCIPLINARY PRACTICE OF CONSERVATION, INCREASES ACCESS TO MUSEUM AND ARCHIVAL COLLECTIONS, AND DEVELOPS CURRENT AND FUTURE LEADERS IN THE VISUAL ARTS. THE FOUNDATION CARRIES OUT ITS WORK IN COLLABORATION WITH THE J. PAUL GETTY MUSEUM, THE GETTY RESEARCH INSTITUTE, AND THE GETTY CONSERVATION INSTITUTE TO ENSURE THAT THE GETTY PROGRAMS ACHIEVE MAXIMUM IMPACT.THE MAJORITY OF THE GETTY FOUNDATION'S GRANTS FOR INSTITUTIONS AND ORGANIZATIONS ARE AWARDED THROUGH SPECIAL INITIATIVES FOCUSED ON A SPECIFIC ISSUE OR REGION. IN A NUMBER OF CASES, APPLICATIONS ARE BY INVITATION RATHER THAN THROUGH OPEN COMPETITION. DEADLINES FOR RECEIPT OF APPLICATION MATERIALS, AS WELL AS GRANT AMOUNTS, VARY. ADDITIONAL INFORMATION, AS WELL AS GRANTS AWARDED REPORTS, IS AVAILABLE ONLINE AND FROM THE GETTY FOUNDATION OFFICE. INQUIRIES AND REQUESTS FOR ADDITIONAL INFORMATION SHOULD BE SENT TO: THE GETTY FOUNDATION 1200 GETTY CENTER DRIVE, SUITE 800 LOS ANGELES, CA 90049-1685 PHONE: 310 440-7320 EMAIL: GETTYFOUNDATION@GETTY.EDU WEB: HTTP://WWW.GETTY.EDU/FOUNDATIONGETTY RESIDENTIAL GRANTSTHE J. PAUL GETTY TRUST MAKES GRANTS TO SCHOLARS, CONSERVATION PROFESSIONALS, AND STUDENTS THROUGH VARIOUS RESIDENTIAL GRANT PROGRAMS, DESCRIBED BELOW. GRANTEES PURSUE THEIR WORK AT THE GETTY CENTER OR THE GETTY VILLA. RESIDENTIAL RESEARCH GRANTSTHE GETTY'S RESIDENTIAL RESEARCH GRANTS ENABLE SCHOLARS AND OTHER SPECIALISTS TO PURSUE INDEPENDENT RESEARCH, COLLABORATE WITH THEIR COLLEAGUES, AND WORK ON SPECIAL SCHOLARLY PROJECTS AT THE GETTY CENTER OR THE GETTY VILLA. GRANTS RELATED TO SPECIFIC THEMES ARE OFFERED TO SCHOLARS AT VARIOUS LEVELS BY THE GETTY RESEARCH INSTITUTE, WHICH ALSO PROVIDES GRANTS FOR SHORT TERMS (FIVE DAYS TO EIGHT WEEKS) IN THE GETTY LIBRARY. IN ADDITION, THE GETTY CONSERVATION INSTITUTE OFFERS SUPPORT FOR PROFESSIONALS AND SCHOLARS IN CONSERVATION AND ALLIED FIELDS TO PURSUE INDEPENDENT RESEARCH AT THE GETTY CENTER OR THE GETTY VILLA.AWARDS ARE MADE THROUGH A COMPETITIVE ONLINE APPLICATION PROCESS, AND GRANT PERIODS VARY FROM ONE MONTH TO TWO YEARS. SELECT SCHOLARS WHO ARE UNIQUELY QUALIFIED TO ADD THEIR KNOWLEDGE AND EXPERTISE TO THE ANNUAL THEME OR SPECIALIZED TOPIC MAY BE INVITED TO ENHANCE A COHORT. THE DEADLINE FOR GETTY RESEARCH INSTITUTE SCHOLARS AND FELLOWS; GETTY CONSERVATION INSTITUTE SCHOLARS, AND J. PAUL GETTY MUSEUM VILLA SCHOLARS AND FELLOWS IS ON OR AROUND OCTOBER 1 EACH YEAR. THE DEADLINE FOR LIBRARY RESEARCH GRANTS IS ON OR AROUND OCTOBER 15.APPLICATIONS ARE RECEIVED ONLINE, AND ADDITIONAL INFORMATION, INCLUDING CURRENT TERMS AND STIPENDS, ARE AVAILABLE THROUGH THE WEBSITE AND FROM: ATTN: GETTY SCHOLAR GRANTS THE GETTY FOUNDATION 1200 GETTY CENTER DRIVE, SUITE 800 LOS ANGELES, CA 90049-1685 PHONE: 310 440-7374 EMAIL: RESEARCHGRANTS@GETTY.EDU WEB: WWW.GETTY.EDU/FOUNDATION/FUNDING/RESIDENTIAL/GETTY_SCHOLARS.HTMLGRADUATE INTERNSHIPSGETTY GRADUATE INTERNSHIPS ARE OFFERED IN THE FOUR PROGRAMS OF THE J. PAUL GETTY TRUST-THE J. PAUL GETTY MUSEUM, THE GETTY RESEARCH INSTITUTE, THE GETTY CONSERVATION INSTITUTE, AND THE GETTY FOUNDATION-AND IN THE TRUST'S PUBLICATIONS AND DIGITAL DEPARTMENTS, TO STUDENTS WHO INTEND TO PURSUE CAREERS IN FIELDS RELATED TO THE VISUAL ARTS. THE GRADUATE INTERNSHIP PROGRAM PROVIDES TRAINING AND WORK EXPERIENCE IN AREAS SUCH AS CURATORIAL, EDUCATION, CONSERVATION, RESEARCH, INFORMATION MANAGEMENT, PUBLIC PROGRAMS, AND GRANT-MAKING FOR TWELVE-MONTH PERIODS. THE DEADLINE FOR GRADUATE INTERNSHIP APPLICATIONS IS ON OR AROUND NOVEMBER 1 OF EACH YEAR. APPLICATIONS ARE RECEIVED ONLINE, AND ADDITIONAL INFORMATION, INCLUDING CURRENT TERMS AND STIPENDS, IS AVAILABLE THROUGH THE WEBSITE AND FROM: ATTN: GRADUATE INTERNSHIP PROGRAM THE GETTY FOUNDATION 1200 GETTY CENTER DRIVE, SUITE 800 LOS ANGELES, CA 90049-1685 PHONE: 310 440-7320 EMAIL: GRADINTERNS@GETTY.EDU WEB: WWW.GETTY.EDU/FOUNDATION/FUNDING/LEADERS/CURRENT/GRAD_INTERNSHIPS.HTMLGETTY POST-BACCALAUREATE CONSERVATION INTERNSHIPSGETTY POST-BACCALAUREATE CONSERVATION INTERNSHIPS ARE FOR TWELVE-MONTH PERIODS AND ARE INTENDED TO SUPPORT POSTBACCALAUREATE STUDENTS WHO ARE COMMITTED TO A CAREER IN CONSERVATION, CAN DEMONSTRATE HAND-SKILLS IN CONSERVATION, POSSESS A STRONG DESIRE TO ATTEND GRADUATE SCHOOL TO FURTHER THAT GOAL, AND WHO ARE MEMBERS OF A GROUP UNDERREPRESENTED IN CAREERS RELATED TO ART CONSERVATION, MUSEUMS, AND/OR VISUAL ARTS ORGANIZATIONS. THIS CAN INCLUDE GROUPS DEFINED BY-AMONG OTHER THINGS-SOCIOECONOMIC STATUS, CULTURAL BACKGROUND, PHYSICAL OR OTHER DISABILITY, GEOGRAPHICAL ORIGIN AND/OR ANY LIFE EXPERIENCES THAT ADD DIVERSE AND UNDERREPRESENTED PERSPECTIVES. THE DEADLINE FOR GETTY POST-BACCALAUREATE CONSERVATION INTERNSHIPS IS ON OR AROUND NOVEMBER 1 OF EACH YEAR. APPLICATIONS ARE RECEIVED ONLINE, AND ADDITIONAL INFORMATION, INCLUDING CURRENT TERMS AND STIPENDS, IS AVAILABLE THROUGH THE WEBSITE AND FROM: 1200 GETTY CENTER DRIVE, SUITE 800 LOS ANGELES, CA 90049-1685 PHONE: 310 440-7320 EMAIL: POSTBACCINTERNS@GETTY.EDU WEB: HTTPS://WWW.GETTY.EDU/PROJECTS/GETTY-POST-BACCALAUREATE-CONSERVATION-INTERNSHIPS/GETTY MARROW UNDERGRADUATE INTERNSHIPS AT GETTYUNDERGRADUATE INTERNSHIPS ARE OFFERED IN VARIOUS PROGRAMS AND DEPARTMENTS OF THE GETTY TRUST FULL-TIME FOR TEN CONSECUTIVE WEEKS DURING THE SUMMER. INTERNSHIPS PROVIDE TRAINING AND WORK EXPERIENCE IN AREAS SUCH AS CONSERVATION, LIBRARY COLLECTIONS, PUBLICATIONS, MUSEUM EDUCATION, CURATORSHIP, GRANTS ADMINISTRATION, PUBLIC PROGRAMS, SITE OPERATIONS, AND INFORMATION TECHNOLOGY. MARROW UNDERGRADUATE INTERNSHIPS AT GETTY ARE INTENDED FOR CURRENTLY ENROLLED UNDERGRADUATES AND RECENT GRADUATES RESIDING OR ATTENDING COLLEGE IN LOS ANGELES COUNTY WHO ARE MEMBERS OF GROUPS UNDERREPRESENTED IN CAREERS RELATED TO MUSEUMS AND/OR VISUAL ARTS ORGANIZATIONS. THIS CAN INCLUDE GROUPS DEFINED BY-AMONG OTHER THINGS-SOCIOECONOMIC STATUS, CULTURAL BACKGROUND, PHYSICAL OR OTHER DISABILITY, GEOGRAPHICAL ORIGIN AND/OR ANY LIFE EXPERIENCES THAT ADD DIVERSE AND UNDERREPRESENTED PERSPECTIVES. THE DEADLINE FOR GETTY MARROW UNDERGRADUATE INTERNSHIPS AT THE GETTY APPLICATIONS IS ON OR AROUND FEBRUARY 1 OF EACH YEAR. APPLICATIONS ARE RECEIVED ONLINE, AND ADDITIONAL INFORMATION, INCLUDING CURRENT TERMS AND STIPENDS, IS AVAILABLE THROUGH THE WEBSITE AND FROM: THE GETTY FOUNDATION 1200 GETTY CENTER DRIVE, SUITE 800 LOS ANGELES, CA 90049-1685 PHONE: 310 440-7320 EMAIL: SUMMERINTERNS@GETTY.EDU WEB: HTTPS://WWW.GETTY.EDU/PROJECTS/GETTY-MARROW-UNDERGRADUATE-INTERNSHIPS/GETTY MARROW UNDERGRADUATE INTERNSHIPS AT L.A. ARTS ORGANIZATIONSGRANTS TO LOS ANGELES-AREA MUSEUMS AND VISUAL ARTS ORGANIZATIONS ARE OFFERED IN ORDER TO FUND INTERNSHIPS FOR THE NEXT GENERATION OF ARTS PROFESSIONALS. THESE INTERNSHIPS ARE DESIGNED TO OFFER ELIGIBLE STUDENTS EXPERIENCE IN KEY AREAS SUCH AS CURATORIAL WORK, CONSERVATION, EDUCATION, PUBLICATIONS, AND RELATED PROGRAMMATIC ACTIVITIES. THE INTERNSHIPS ARE INTENDED FOR CURRENTLY ENROLLED UNDERGRADUATES AND RECENT GRADUATES RESIDING OR ATTENDING COLLEGE IN LOS ANGELES WHO ARE MEMBERS OF GROUPS CURRENTLY UNDERREPRESENTED IN CAREERS RELATED TO MUSEUMS AND/OR VISUAL ARTS ORGANIZATION. THIS CAN INCLUDE GROUPS DEFINED BY-AMONG OTHER THINGS-SOCIOECONOMIC STATUS, CULTURAL BACKGROUND, PHYSICAL OR OTHER DISABILITY, GEOGRAPHICAL ORIGIN AND/OR ANY LIFE EXPERIENCES THAT ADD DIVERSE AND UNDERREPRESENTED PERSPECTIVES. THE DEADLINE FOR GETTY MARROW UNDERGRADUATE INTERNSHIPS AT L.A. ORGANIZATIONS APPLICATIONS IS ON OR AROUND NOVEMBER 15 OF EACH YEAR. APPLICATIONS ARE RECEIVED ONLINE AND ADDITIONAL INFORMATION INCLUDING CURRENT TERMS AND STIPENDS, IS AVAILABLE THROUGH THE WEBSITE AND FROM: THE GETTY FOUNDATION 1200 GETTY CENTER DRIVE, SUITE 800 LOS ANGELES, CA 90049-1685 PHONE: (310) 440-7320 EMAIL: SUMMERINTERNS@GETTY.EDU WEB: HTTPS://WWW.GETTY.EDU/PROJECTS/GETTY-MARROW-UNDERGRADUATE-INTERNSHIPS/GETTY INVESTMENT INTERNSHIPSTHE GETTY INVESTMENTS DEPARTMENT OFFERS A FULL-TIME 10-WEEK INTENSIVE AND HANDS-ON SUMMER INTERNSHIP PROGRAM THAT PROVIDES UNDERGRADUATE STUDENTS THE OPPORTUNITY TO LEARN ABOUT FINANCIAL INVESTMENTS IN THE NONPROFIT SECTOR AND GAIN VALUABLE HANDS-ON EXPERIENCE THEY CAN APPLY TOWARDS A FUTURE CAREER. THE PROGRAM ENCOURAGES APPLICANTS FROM GROUPS UNDERREPRESENTED IN CAREERS RELATED TO FINANCE AND INVESTMENT MANAGEMENT. THIS CAN INCLUDE GROUPS DEFINED BY-AMONG OTHER THINGS-SOCIOECONOMIC STATUS, CULTURAL BACKGROUND, PHYSICAL OR OTHER DISABILITY, GEOGRAPHICAL ORIGIN, AND/OR ANY LIFE EXPERIENCES THAT ADD DIVERSE AND UNDERREPRESENTED PERSPECTIVES.THE DEADLINE FOR GETTY INVESTMENT INTERNSHIPS IS ON OR AROUND NOVEMBER 1 EACH YEAR. APPLICATIONS ARE ACCEPTED ONLINE AND ADDITIONAL INFORMATION INCLUDING CURRENT TERMS AND STIPENDS, IS AVAILABLE VIA: EMAIL: INVESTMENTS-INTERNSHIP@GETTY.EDUWEB: HTTPS://WWW.GETTY.EDU/PROJECTS/INVESTMENTS-INTERNSHIP/J. PAUL GETTY MUSEUM GUEST SCHOLARSTHE GETTY MUSEUM OFFERS TEN-WEEK TO THREE-MONTH RESIDENTIAL APPOINTMENTS TO INVITED GUEST SCHOLARS WORKING ON PROJECTS RELATED TO THE MUSEUM'S COLLECTIONS OR THOSE OF NEIGHBORIN
